content: The human brain’s active learning memory mechanism, where encoding and retrieval are constantly updated to guide future learning, can explain why 2024-2025 state-of-the-art curiosity-driven active learning agents underperform in long-horizon tasks: they fail to replicate the brain’s practice of prioritizing updating inconsistent episodic memory traces over just storing new novel observations. This creates a testable hypothesis that modifying curious active learning agents to prune and reconsolidate conflicting episodic memory traces (mirroring human memory reactivation during active learning) will improve zero-shot transfer to unseen long-horizon tasks.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
